text: Alkonost: Yeah, my sister cries a lot, but our eyes are still really similar. Her's are a bit calmer, maybe.
Alkonost: Yeah, my sister cries a lot, but our eyes are still really similar. They're not red, is what I mean.
Alkonost: My sister isn't in any shape to take care of herself right now. So my hands are always full.
Alkonost: We've always had strong teeth in our family.
Alkonost: Back when I was a teenager, I even tried to open a beer with them.
Alkonost: Now, I don't buy into all this flapdoodle aura business.
Alkonost: Everything we do, everywhere we end up—that's by our own choice. Not any of that hocus-pocus applesauce.
Alkonost: I'll show you. But please don't bother my sister, okay? She's too tired for this.
Alkonost: Wait... What's the Visitor sign again?
Alkonost: What are you looking for?
Alt: Oh, my eyes? I like them almost as much as my tattoos.
Alt: Got dealt a bad hand with the nails.
Alt: I called a cab to get to the outskirts in peace.
Alt: I'm warning you, I'm a real business shark.
Alt: Oh wow, you've got a camera too.
Alt: I don't mind showing my armpits.
Alt: It's just…not a nice sight.
Anger: Whatever. Gaze into ‘em, if that's what you want.
Anger: I don't give a fuck. Here.
Anger: I don't give a flying fuck.
Anxiety: You want to check my eyes?
Anxiety: I can already guess what you'll see...
Anxiety: What happens if I have one of the signs?
Anxiety: Y-You wanna look at my fingernails? That's a weird thing to check...
Anxiety: But okay... Take a look...
Anxiety: W-Wait what—you're gonna make a decision based on my teeth? O-Okay...
Anxiety: I just had my teeth fixed.
Anxiety: I can't really remember being at the dentist. Was I ever even there?
Anxiety: God, I'm so nervous, I can't even remember if I ate yesterday.
Anxiety: I-If it turns out I'm a Visitor...does that mean I'll start killing people?
Anxiety: Will I be dangerous? Or...was I always dangerous?
Anxiety: Maybe... Maybe I already kill people?
Anxiety: Just like those Visitors back home...
Anxiety: ...Sorry, it was a horrible experience. I'll show you.
Anxiety: Wh-Why are there so many signs?
Anxiety: WHY DO I FIT EVERY SINGLE ONE OF THEM?! HAHAHA!
Bald: You probably noticed, I’ve got this crappy thing on my eye.
Bald: I've, uh…got eczema. That’s why there’s a bandage on my arm.
Bald: Uh…sure. I smoke sometimes. Hope I don’t stink too much.
Bald: Oh, I love that. Roll camera!
Bald: It’s just another skin thing. See? I’m bald, and I’ve got patches like that all over.
Bald: Shit, I haven’t showered in forever. My bad.
Ballerina: Aaaaah, now to most interesting part.
Ballerina: I have things written in pupils. See...?
Ballerina: I dance. Why interrupt to look at dancey hands?
Ballerina: You chew up what conscience leaves behind?
Ballerina: What is? I do not know.
Ballerina: HAHAHAHA! Stare. Sniff. What else?
Bestson: Oh... Is it really that bad? Well...okay...
Bestson: You wanna take a picture? Well...okay. Just...tell me that everything's fine afterwards, okay?
BigLebowski: Watch those hands. One wrong move and you're losing an eye.
BigLebowski: Whatever. Just don't freak out, alright?
BigLebowski: It's an allergic reaction. I break out in itchy rashes. Nasty stuff. Had it since I was a lil kid.
BigLebowski: Weird thing to ask. And hey, you try anything and I'll bust your face in. For real.
BigLebowski: If you're planning on sending this photo somewhere to help my parents track me down, you can fuck right off.
BigLebowski: Urgh. Whatever. I don't care.
Blind: By the way...they are the last witnesses.
Blind: Go ahead, use the flash. I don't care.
Buddy: Ah. Alright, snap a pic if you gotta.
Buddy: Uh, buddy... You sure about this? I mean, go ahead.
Buddy: Here they are... Just plain ol' ears, nothin' fancy.
Cold: Now... Eyes are a window to hell, not to the s-soul.
Cold: My soul b-burned out long ago. Maybe that's why I'm so c-c-cold.
Cold: N-Nails? Sure, I'll show you.
Cold: Hm. I t-tore them all off.
Cold: Still, you can t-take a look.
Cold: What does a photograph p-prove?
Cold: Does it not s-seem strange to you?
Cold: B-B-But...it's not up to me. Is it?
Cold: I... I need a m-moment.
Cold: Our b-bodies are nothing but holes.
Cold: Maybe that's why p-p-people vanish so easily? S-Swallowed up the moment you turn away.
Couple: Sure, show me yours and I'll show you mine!
Couple: Sorry... I'm a little nervous... But who wouldn't be? Being tested is terrifying, even when you've got nothing to hide.
Couple: Yepadoodle! My wife and I keep clean, don't we, honey?
Couple: To protect the ones you love, sometimes you gotta sink your teeth into life, haha.
Couple: But... I guess that might give you reason to suspect us.
Couple: Hold on, let me think...
Couple: Fine. Take the damn picture. But I get to keep it after.
Couple: Oh, no, no, no. I'll be the only one showing you those, buster!
Couple: I don't see how that has anything to do with the Visitors.
Couple: But sure, I've got nothing to hide. Here.
Couple: And? What do you see?
Couple: Honey, you ever hear anything about ears being a sign of a Visitor? Quiet as a mouse, you are!
Couple: Oh, really? Hm. I see... Okay.
Cultist1: If that is what you decree.
Cultist3: These eyes of mine now hardly see.
Cultist3: These make us look like the Outsiders.
Cultist3: Amusing how the lower and upper jaws can be so different.
Cultist3: I shall attempt to shift my robe. Please be patient.
Cultist2: Sleep lays beyond our reach.
Cultist2: Crooked as a soul...and perhaps larger than advisable.
Cultist2: A picture...? This is new.
Daughter: You're gonna look inside my eyes?
Daughter: Will you see what I'm thinking about?
Daughter: In that case, I'll make sure to think really carefully, don't worry.
Daughter: I can cut my own nails already.
Daughter: I'm not very good at it yet...
Daughter: My teeth? You want me to show you my teeth?
Daughter: Why? I still have some baby teeth. Dad said...
Daughter: I'm not gonna cry anymore.
Daughter: I don't have many pictures of me.
Daughter: Will you show me it after?
Daughter: Daddy said you have to be careful of ticks and bugs. Is that what you're looking for?
Doctor: Of course, I'll show you my eyes.
Doctor: Not sure if you noticed, I've got polycoria.
Doctor: It's an official diagnosis, yes.
Doctor: Yes, these hands have performed countless surgeries.
Doctor: I could tell you operating table stories for days...but maybe another time.
Doctor: For now, please, take a look at them.
Doctor: Thank you for your concern, but my teeth are in perfect health.
Doctor: Ah, you mean to check for signs of being a Visitor.
Doctor: Of course, take a look.
Doctor: You have to thoroughly check every little thing. I can only assume this must be part of it.
Doctor: Yes, just a moment. I'm not as quick as I used to be.
Doctor: Fair enough. I trust you know what you're doing.
Dude: Can't keep my lids open too long. Looking at stuff stresses me out, man.
Dude: Dude, I was, like, diggin' up a geocache recently. Dirt? Don't sweat it.
Dude: Hold up, bro...gotta watch the strip show first.
Edgar: Even the memory of her eyes unsettles me so... Forgive me, you wished to see my own...
Edgar: She had such tender hands. At times I feared they would dissolve into mine, like she was a ghost…
Edgar: I remember how pearls shone in her mouth... How I cherished her smile...
Esenin: Every day is more ridiculous than the last.
Esenin: What if I hadn't slept? Or was crying?
Esenin: I don't understand how this works at all anymore.
Esenin: And what are you going to do if one sign confirms it? Or several?
Esenin: Fingernails caked with dirt are a sign?
Esenin: They could just wash their hands!
Esenin: You're saying a gardener would automatically become a Visitor, then?
Esenin: You want me to show you my teeth?
Esenin: Okay, take a look. Take in that odor of sadness and smoke.
Esenin: Ever get the sense that it's all leading to some kind of terrifying hell?
Esenin: Everything's supposedly under control, but there's complete chaos all around us.
Esenin: Well, take your damn photo.
Esenin: Fucking seriously? Are you a moron?
Esenin: Has FEMA blended your brains?
Esenin: Or are you just blasted? I thought all the strong stuff was gone already...
Esenin: Well, since you're asking, I'll show you!
Esenin: Do you even know what you're looking for?
Esenin: What to watch out for?
Esenin: Watching you fumble around has really driven home just how royally fucked we are.
Experienced: Wanna see if I got a conscience in there?
Experienced: Alright, then take a look.
Experienced: No problem, mate. Take a look.
Experienced: The screws ain’t exactly handin’ out minty toothbrushes. Don’t freak about the bleeding gums.
Experienced: ...Or is this some kinda test?
Experienced: Feels like I’m back gettin’ logged. Photo goin’ in my file and all that.
Experienced: Fine, snap it if you gotta.
Experienced: Mate, look... I’ll show ya, but...
Experienced: Back in the day, I got burned fuckin' around drunk. So don’t freak out.
Experienced: You really need to see those?
Fanatic: LOOK INTO MY DARK EYES!
Fanatic: I use them to absorb all the benefits of the righteous beams, transforming them into energy.
Fanatic: I get all of my energy through my EYES!
Fanatic: As you wish, my friend!
Fanatic: Behold these holy nails as much as you like.
Fanatic: I've trained myself to absorb the beneficial sunlight with them.
Fanatic: And open beer bottles, of course!
Fanatic: You want me to show you my teeth?
Fanatic: MY TEETH STOOD UP FOR THE TRUTH!
Fanatic: ...and they fell for it. Martyrs.
Fanatic: You can see that for yourself.
Fanatic: You wish to immortalize me on film?
Fanatic: Did you know I once shook hands with the SHINING SUN LEADER himself?
Fanatic: It was I who taught him how to stare straight into the Sun. But I'll gift you that holy tale later, human.
Fanatic: Go on, GRACE OUR EYES WITH MY IMMORTALIZATION!
Fanatic: Well, give them a sniff if that's what you're into.
Fanatic: This is the result of self-care.
Fanatic: Well, give them a sniff if that's what you're into.
Fanatic: Yes! I receive the righteous signals of LIFE straight through my ears! How did you know?
Fatman: What's next, you want me to bend over for you? What a joke, wasting a minister's time with this crap.
Fatman: You just won't let it go, will you?
Fatman: Go on. Just lemme get up... Like the good old days...
Fatman: Hmph. Some funny old tests you've got here.
Firefighter: Sure, whatever. You'll only get a charred corpse, though. That's how it always is.
Firefighter: You sure? It ain't pretty sight...
Firefighter: You won't find much to check. They're charred down to the cartilage.
Foreigner: Dszg? Nb MOUF? Bvzs, rg dzh hvdm hsfg.  Hl dszg mld? Wl R mvvw gl SMYAL?
Foreigner: Dsviv rh gsv kslgl? Gsrh wvhvievh gl yv uiznvw!
Foreigner: R wlmg ivzoob tvg zmb lu gsrh... yfg dszgvevi...
FormerFema: Ah, yes. I love this one. People's eyes are beautiful after all.
FormerFema: You want me to take off my gloves? No way. Not safe. You'll have to test me like this.
FormerFema: So, you actually take your job seriously.
FormerFema: I need to warn you, it might look...unusual.
FormerFema: You really want to see this?
FormerFema: You kidding? We didn't train for this! Don't know about others, but our team never checked for this.
FortuneTeller: Oh, my eyes? They're brown, of course.
FortuneTeller: Well, go ahead and look.
FortuneTeller: Be careful now. My aura can be blinding!
FortuneTeller: Ohhh, the things the universe puts in my path... Very well, take a look.
FortuneTeller: Go ahead and look, but be careful. My ears hear everything—even your thoughts.
Fugitive: Go ahead. They've taken a beating, that's for sure.
Fugitive: Dirty, yeah? Fell in a goddamn puddle running from those pigs. Before that, cut 'em climbing over some barbed wire.
Fugitive: Yeah? Didn't think anyone still did that.
Fugitive: Well, shit... You're not screwing with me, are ya?
Fugitive: Seriously? Don't ya think these "signs" are meant to spread more panic?
Fugitive: Dirty? Yeah they're dirty. Like anyone's would be, living and working without taking any showers.
Fugitive: Seriously? You think you can spot a Visitor by their ears? Alright, be my fuckin' guest.
FunnyGuy: Got a dark spot on my iris.
FunnyGuy: Just your regular work-worn hands.
FunnyGuy: Aw, come on. Brush too hard and you'll wear down the enamel.
FunnyGuy: Oh, guess I should button up.
FunnyGuy: Eh, screw it. It's just one picture.
FunnyGuy: Our plant's a real lawsuit waiting to happen.
FunnyGuy: I was supposed to retire soon.
GraveDigger: These hands fed me, kept me alive.
GraveDigger: When you dig too deep... sometimes you don't just find bones.
GraveDigger: A photo's a dangerous thing, I tell ya! Like a mirror. It keeps what it sees.
GraveDigger: Ugh, well, aren't you something...?
GraveDigger: Just ears. Ain't nothin' fancy.
Greatmother: Darlin', I've always had busted vessels in my eyes.
Greatmother: Don't see how you reckon you'll figure I'm a Visitor with that.
Greatmother: That one ain't gone work...
Greatmother: Growin' old ain't a joy, darlin'.
Greatmother: Bless your heart! What teeth, darlin'?
Greatmother: Go on, take yourself a good ol' look!
Greatmother: Ain't nobody taken a photo of Momma in years!
Greatmother: Go on, darlin'. Why not?
Greatmother: Momma's gettin' old, darlin'. My body just ain't what it used to be.
Greatmother: At night I hear this scratchin'. Like somethin's crawlin' 'round underneath us.
Hunter: But after that last run-in, they ain't nothing but red.
Hunter: I was taking down some varmint and he threw dirt, or shit, or SOMETHIN' into my eyes. Hurts all the damn time now.
Hunter: Easy, kid. That smarts somethin' fierce.
Hunter: A life like mine'll drain the color from any fella's eyes.
Hunter: Let's move this along, kid.
Hunter: Teeth, huh? Fine, I'll show ya.
Hunter: Take pictures of somethin' else. Kill some varmint. I don't give a hoot...just get your darn camera outta my face!
Hunter: More signs, more easier we can fine those varmints.
Hunter: Heard that report too, kid. ...Fine, take a look.
Intruder: I heard somewhere that insomnia makes your eyes darker.
Intruder: I wonder if that's true?
Intruder: These hands are ready to push through any thing in my way.
Intruder: In that way, maybe I'm clawing my way into my own dreams?
Intruder: Yes. I'll show you in a moment.
Intruder: I'm so tired, I tend to forget things.
Intruder: I think we need to put less focus on the little things. Make all of this seem a little less...odd.
Intruder: I may have a bad memory, but I still remember to brush my teeth.
Intruder: I'm curious to see what it looks like.
Intruder: For some reason they hurt, like they're irritated. Odd...
Intruder: Hmm? Haven't heard of that one before.
Intruder: Why not, let's give it a try.
Jacket: I’ll show you my eyes, just…
Jacket: Honestly, I’m a little nervous. Don’t judge too much.
Jacket: I don’t mind taking a photo, but your camera looks kinda weird.
Jacket: Ears weren’t on the list of signs, were they?
Jacket: Though I’ll admit, I am not particularly aware what the signs are. Maybe I just missed that one.
Jacob: God forbid you show this to anyone in FEMA.
Jacob: Anything else you want me to show you?
Jacob: Don't you think these checks are kinda stupid?
Leper: Yeah, my skins bad there too.
Leper: A photo? That’s a sign?
Leper: What? What the hell do you mean, ears?
Leper: No offense, but I know the Visitor signs and that’s not one of them.
Leper: But if you really wanna see my ears, go ahead.
Luka: Didn't know that mattered too.
Luka: Our voices are lost in the void of the universe.
Luka: But I'm always ready to listen to you!
Marauder: Alrighty. I'll show ya, and I'll tell ya the story too.
Marauder: Fine, fine. Your house, your rules...
Marauder: Damn... and here I thought I was the one who didn't know how to get by in polite society...
Marauder: They're not torn or chewed up like some stray mog. Just regular ol' ears.
Mother: I haven't cleaned them in a long time. Might well be blocked up by now.
Nervous: I've learned to stop picking at my nails.
Nervous: Those meds made my teeth a little thinny-winny...
Nervous: They never taught me how to take care of myself.
Nervous: But I saw how they did a little wishy-washy in the hospital.
Nervous: I don't give them any attention.
NotTrue: Oh, deary... Are you sure you want that?
NotTrue: The eyes are like windows. For light in houses, and for truth in people.
NotTrue: But mine are dusty... The glass is cracked... Moths ate the curtains long ago...
NotTrue: Don't look, deary. There's nothing there.
NotTrue: You're wastin' that film on lil ol' me? Well, I'll be!
NotTrue: Feh! Making an old woman lift her arms... Have you no shame?
NotTrue: Well, what can I do? You are the owner of the fine house.
NotTrue: Mercy me! Now that's quite the notion.
Nun: Well, go on then. Go on.
Nun: Photography? Pah! I have no time to waste on such nonsense!
Nun: ...I see you shall grant me no peace.
Nun: Well then... If it is His will, let it be done.
Nun: You want to check my ears?! How very dare you!
Provocateur: And what are you even looking for, hm? Redness?
Provocateur: And what are you hoping to find there, hm? An evil stare?
Provocateur: Maybe you'd like to see if there are any leftovers you can scavenge while you're in there, hm?
Provocateur: Want me to say cheese for you, too? Hm?
Provocateur: Oh, believe anything the TV says, do we?
Provocateur: What if they say Visitors all have pubic hair, hm? Will we need to drop our pants for your unconstitutional inspections?
Raskolnikov: Oh... I mean, yeah. Do what you gotta do, man. Go ahead, take a look.
Raskolnikov: Yeah... They're kinda gnarly. I-I used to try and hide them, but... No point anymore.
Raskolnikov: Oh... Teeth? Yeah, sure sure.
Raskolnikov: Why do you need my picture?
Raskolnikov: H-Hey, no need to point that gun at me, man! Okay, okay, take my photo! Jeez...
Raskolnikov: Aw jeez... A-Are you sure this is necessary?
Raskolnikov: O-Oh, yeah, sure. But why ears? I hope you're not expecting anything...awful.
Unstable: Still get flashbacks from that wild ride.
Rocker: Yeeeah… These teeth have soaked up way too much smoke.
Rocker: Hell, let’s take a photo together. Maybe I'll sign it!
Rocker: I used to stretch my ears. Then… I got bored with it.
Scammer: My eyes have been itchy as balls lately.
Scammer: You think it's from the sun? Kinda sus right?
Scammer: Da fuck, bro? I'm just a regular guy.
Scammer: I don't do no crap with my nails, man, see?
Scammer: My homie was a dentist.
Scammer: Guess what? They knocked his fucking teeth out! HAHAHA!
Scammer: Oh, you wanna see them. Aight...
Scammer: Wanna snap a pic together? Aight.
Scammer: I'm pretty sensitive to light, f'real.
Scammer: You picked a freaky hustle this time, douchebag.
Scammer: Hey, hands off my hustle, bro!
Scammer: You take a peek, tell 'em they've got a Visitor Spirit in 'em, then charge 'em crazy cash to keep your mouth shut. Boom!
Scammer: I'm down with it, cause we're bros, bro. But don't try to pull none of that sus shit on me, yeah?
FatalDame: Eyes can give away a lot about how a person thinks.
FatalDame: They say nothing compares to holding the hands of someone you love...
FatalDame: Do you like what you see?
FatalDame: Sorry, baby... I'm tired of posing...
FatalDame: Let's move on to some more...physical...checks, shall we?
FatalDame: Of course, baby. I can't never refuse you...
FatalDame: Mm, is this your fetish, baby?
Sirin: My sister and I have similar eyes.
Sirin: I don't care if anything's left after.
Sirin: Do whatever you think you should.
Sirin: My sister shaves my armpits. She takes care of me.
Sirin: All I have left is an earring.
Sirin: It reminds me of my parents.
TaxiDriver: My eyes are always tired. Hard to keep 'em shut though.
TaxiDriver: I'm just used to... watchin' everything. Who's drivin' behind me, who's starin', who's stayin' eerily quiet...
TaxiDriver: Work habits. Can't shake 'em. So I kinda got insomnia now.
TaxiDriver: Alright... They ain't exactly pretty, though.
TaxiDriver: Teeth are just teeth, you know? Nothin' special.
TaxiDriver: I always kinda kept an eye on mine. Even whitened 'em when I was a kid.
TaxiDriver: Go on, man. I'm ready.
TaxiDriver: My ears? They're just like anybody else's. Nothin' interestin' about 'em.
Teacher: Are... Are you sure this is really necessary? These confounded signs...
Theorist: Ah, the eyes. Yes, of course.
Theorist: Armpits, you say? ...Now that's unusual.
Tough: I've seen a lot of shit in my life.
Tough: I'm sure it's all right there, if you care to look.
Tough: Nobody ever says it to me, but it’s nice to feel that way for myself.
Tough: My canines stick out, they've been that way since I was a kid.
Tough: I got teased for it a lot. Not anymore.
Tough: I don’t take pictures often. But sure, let’s see what comes out.
Tough: Just got my ears pierced, actually.
Tourist: My hands? You're looking for some kind of rash?
Tourist: You mean…teeth can show if I caught the virus?
Tourist: Is that some kind of government tech to detect infections?
Tourist: If that's what this is, I'm happy to try it.
Tourist: Oh, that makes sense. What if the virus spreads through the ear canal?
Unstable: I saw the report. The eyes should be a kinda dark, murky green.
Unstable: Don’t mix it up though, there’s lots of types of green in the world these days.
Unstable: I kinda get itchy a lot, like ants running all over... Ha.
Unstable: Don't think I've been picking at anything, so my hands should be fine.
Unstable: Did I tell you I can taste Enerjeka even when I’m not drinking it?
Unstable: Feels like it's made of some mad chemicals.
Unstable: I only ever got my pictures taken at parties.
Unstable: People don’t really throw parties anymore. Or maybe they just don’t invite me. Ha.
Unstable: Ah, the only sign that makes sense.
Wheelchair: Where do you want me to look? Right at you?
Wheelchair: Hey, I'm just grateful I still even HAVE hands.
Wheelchair: If I didn't, I wouldn't be able to race!
Wheelchair: You wanna take a photo of me?
Wheelchair: Not really the biggest fan of being photographed, but whatever.
Wheelchair: Sure thing, I guess... HAHAHAHA, okay, damn, that's good!
Wheelchair: So you picked up that broadcast, too?
Widow: What are you looking for? Redness?
Widow: They're worn out from carrying the one they refused to save.
Widow: No one helped me save my husband, and now...
Widow: YOU WANT TO MEMORIALIZE HIS DEATH?
Widow: I have no words left.
Widow: Just do what you want and leave me alone.
Widow: Please tell me, ARE YOU INSANE?!
Widow: If those devils told you to jump off a cliff, would you be the first over the edge?
Widow: You... For fuck's— I have no words.
Widow: What do you want from me?
Widow: Really? You'd go that far...?
Widow: I don't know what to tell you.
Wifefema: It's so damn dry here... That's why I'm blinking a lot.
Wifefema: If you insist... Just don't go showin' my hubby, now.
Wifefema: You can put that look back in your pocket, mister. You asked for it!
Wolfhound: Looking to find a stranger in me? Eh?
Wolfhound: Not afraid you'll see yourself in there?
Wolfhound: Yeah, they ain't clean. Killed some mug just the other day, actually.
Wolfhound: They tried to kill me first, mate. Like I'd just stand there waitin' like a muppet.
Wolfhound: Me? I hit back, sunshine.
Wolfhound: Get too close and I'll have to sink these teeth right into your neck.
Wolfhound: I don't appreciate bein' photographed, sunshine.
Wolfhound: If I find out those photos've ended up anywhere but the rubbish, you and me'll be 'aving words.
Wolfhound: That, I don't respect, sunshine. And I'll remember it.
Wolfhound: What next? "My, what big teeth you 'ave!" This is mental.
